steps.title: Find the one-unit factor
steps.content: For $f(x)=k(1.72)^x$, increasing $x$ by $1$ multiplies the output by $1.72$. So the same percent increase corresponds to a factor of $1.72$.

steps.title: Make four units act like one
steps.content: For $g(x)$ to have that same factor when $x$ increases by $4$, its exponent must increase by $1$ when $x$ increases by $4$. The expression $$ k(1.72^x)^{\frac{1}{4}}=k(1.72)^{x/4} $$ does that because $\frac{x+4}{4}=\frac{x}{4}+1$. The answer is A.
